_description: Departmental / Internal. Organization with hard boundaries between departments. No self-registration — all users are invited by department admins. Departments cannot see each other's data. Best for: enterprise internal tools, government systems, healthcare (HIPAA), financial services, any system requiring strict data isolation.
_examples: Hospital system — ER, Radiology, Pharmacy each isolated with role-based access
Enterprise ERP — Finance, HR, Engineering see only their own data
Government agency — divisions with classified data boundaries

service.name: My Enterprise System
service.description: Internal enterprise system with departmental isolation

tiers.description: Root org — global admin, permission schema, API keys. No direct users except platform admins.
Department org — hard isolation boundary. Each department has its own admin, users, and permission subset.

isolation._wall_note: Departments are fully isolated. No cross-department data access. A user in Finance cannot see Engineering data even if they have the same permission names — the Zanzibar check scopes to their org.

hosted_login._note: No self-registration. All users are provisioned by department admins via invite.

onboarding._note: Platform admin creates departments and assigns first department_admin. Department admins invite their own users.

service_accounts._note: Platform-level service accounts for inter-system integration (ERP connectors, reporting pipelines, monitoring).

delegation._note: Department admins can delegate approval authority during absence. Scoped to permissions the delegator holds.

sso._note: Each department can have its own IdP or share the corporate IdP. JIT provisioning auto-creates users from SAML assertions.

teams._note: Cross-functional teams within departments (e.g., project teams, review committees, on-call rotations).

_zero_config_note: Invite-only with hard isolation. No self-registration, no cross-department visibility. Permissions are scoped to the department org — same permission name in two departments does NOT mean shared access.
